---
title: "DESeq2 Basic Two-Group Differential Expression"
category: "RNA-seq"
subcategory: "Differential Expression"
packages: "DESeq2, dplyr, ggplot2"
r_version: "4.4.0"
package_versions: "DESeq2 1.42.0, dplyr 1.1.4, ggplot2 3.5.0"
author: "Joash Joshua Ayo"
date: "2026-04-04"
difficulty: "Beginner"
---

## Overview
This script performs a standard two-group differential expression analysis using DESeq2.
It takes a raw count matrix and a metadata file, runs the DESeq2 pipeline, and outputs
a results table of differentially expressed genes (DEGs).

## Use Case
Use this when you have RNA-seq count data (genes x samples) and want to compare
gene expression between two conditions (e.g. treated vs. control, disease vs. healthy).

## Input
- `counts.csv` — Raw integer count matrix. Rows = genes (Ensembl ID or gene symbol),
  columns = sample names. Must NOT be normalized (DESeq2 needs raw counts).
- `metadata.csv` — Sample metadata with at least two columns:
  - `sample` — must match column names in counts exactly
  - `condition` — the grouping variable (e.g. "control", "treated")

## Required Packages
```r
# Install if needed:
# if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager")) install.packages("BiocManager")
# BiocManager::install("DESeq2")
# install.packages("dplyr")
# install.packages("ggplot2")

library(DESeq2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

## Code
```r
# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Load data
# ----------------------------------------------------------
counts   <- read.csv("counts.csv",   row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
metadata <- read.csv("metadata.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Ensure sample order matches between counts and metadata
metadata <- metadata[match(colnames(counts), metadata$sample), ]

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Create DESeq2 object
# ----------------------------------------------------------
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = counts,
  colData   = metadata,
  design    = ~ condition      # change 'condition' to your column name
)

# Set the reference level (the control/baseline group)
dds$condition <- relevel(dds$condition, ref = "control")   # change "control" to your baseline

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Pre-filter low-count genes
# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Keep genes with at least 10 counts in 2 or more samples
keep <- rowSums(counts(dds) >= 10) >= 2
dds  <- dds[keep, ]
cat("Genes after filtering:", nrow(dds), "\n")

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Run DESeq2
# ----------------------------------------------------------
dds <- DESeq(dds)

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 5: Extract results
# ----------------------------------------------------------
res <- results(dds,
               alpha        = 0.05,   # FDR threshold
               lfcThreshold = 0)      # minimum fold change (set to e.g. 1 for 2-fold)

# Summary of significant genes
summary(res)

# Convert to data frame and sort by adjusted p-value
res_df <- as.data.frame(res) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  filter(!is.na(padj))

# Add significance label
res_df$significant <- ifelse(res_df$padj < 0.05 & abs(res_df$log2FoldChange) > 1,
                             "Yes", "No")

cat("\nNumber of significant DEGs (padj < 0.05, |LFC| > 1):",
    sum(res_df$significant == "Yes"), "\n")

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 6: Save results
# ----------------------------------------------------------
write.csv(res_df, "DESeq2_results.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Step 7: Quick volcano plot
# ----------------------------------------------------------
ggplot(res_df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj), color = significant)) +
  geom_point(size = 1.2, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("No" = "#94A3B8", "Yes" = "#2563EB")) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed", color = "#64748B") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "#64748B") +
  labs(title = "Volcano Plot — DESeq2",
       x     = "Log2 Fold Change",
       y     = "-Log10 Adjusted P-value") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

## Output
- `DESeq2_results.csv` — Full results table with columns:
  `baseMean`, `log2FoldChange`, `lfcSE`, `stat`, `pvalue`, `padj`, `significant`
- A volcano plot showing the distribution of DEGs
- Console summary of up/down/non-significant gene counts

## Common Errors & Fixes

**Error:** `"colnames(countData) == rownames(colData) are not all TRUE"`  
**Fix:** Sample names in your counts columns must exactly match the `sample` column in metadata (case-sensitive, no spaces).

**Error:** `"object 'condition' not found"`  
**Fix:** Make sure the column name in `design = ~ condition` matches a real column in your metadata CSV.

**Error:** `"less than 2 samples per group"`  
**Fix:** DESeq2 needs at least 2 samples per group. Check your metadata for typos in the condition column.

**Warning:** `"some variables in design formula are characters, converting to factors"`  
**Fix:** This is safe to ignore, but you can silence it with `metadata$condition <- as.factor(metadata$condition)` before creating dds.

## References
- Love MI, Huber W, Anders S (2014). Moderated estimation of fold change and
  dispersion for RNA-seq data with DESeq2. *Genome Biology*, 15:550.
  https://doi.org/10.1186/s13059-014-0550-8
- DESeq2 Bioconductor vignette:
  https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/vignettes/DESeq2/inst/doc/DESeq2.html
```
